title: Chiptune: Writing NES Music on the 2A03
subtitle: MUS-8B · A 3-week chiptune composition intensive
description: Go from never having opened a tracker to writing, mixing, and exporting a finished NES chiptune that plays on real hardware. A composition course taught through the Ricoh 2A03: the two pulse channels and their duty cycles, the volume-less triangle, the noise generator, and DPCM samples, then the techniques a generation of composers invented to fit a whole arrangement into five monophonic voices (arpeggio for chords, a second channel for echo, a drum kit built from hiss). Finishes with groove, transitions, mixing inside a non-linear hardware mixer, a tour of the Famicom expansion chips, and export to NSF. Taught through daily listening, lectures, tracker work, quizzes, and three projects that ladder into a capstone track.

lessons.title: Field Guide: The Chip & the Tracker
Days 1–7: The 2A03, the Tracker, Pulse, Instruments, Triangle, Noise
Days 8–14: The Voice Budget, Arpeggio, Echo, Pitch Effects, Drums, Song Form
Days 15–21: Groove, Transitions, Mixing, Expansion Chips, Style Study, Export
